title: SHELTER SERVICES - 211info
text: CALL TO SAFETY SHELTER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter list with bed availability for domestic violence shelters. Not all shelters are listed. Shelter access is dependent on available bed space and is not guaranteed. The agency does not maintain waitlists. Provides referrals for a Safety and Stabilization Assessment (SSA) for domestic violence shelters or housing resources. SSA is required before accessing most direct domestic violence services. Confidential Address Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and/or sexual violence, stalking, sex trafficking, or sex industry workers that are currently needing to flee a dangerous situation and have no safe place to stay for the night. Domestic and/or sexual violence must have occurred within the last six months. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 235-5333 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Hotline (503) 235-5333 Support line Toll-free (888) 235-5333 Support line Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call, text, email, or visit website for more information. Website offers real-time chat support. If receiving a voice mail prompt when calling, provide all of the following: name, number, and verification that it is safe to receive a callback. Safety and Stabilization Assessment (SSA) is completed through the Gateway Center for Domestic Violence Services FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: Domestic Violence Shelters Domestic Violence Shelters * Families with Children Domestic Violence Shelters * Hispanic/Latino Community Emergency Shelter Clearinghouses * Domestic Violence Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: DONOR SERVICES - CELL PHONE PROJECT INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES LEGAL ASSISTANCE MODALITIES - SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CALL TO SAFETY
